member.name: M:VirtoCommerce.Platform.BackupRestore.BackupRestoreManager.IsLikelyCryptoFailure(System.Exception)
member.summary: SharpZipLib doesn't expose a stable, typed "bad password" exception — different versions emit `ZipException("Wrong password")`, `ZipException("invalid CRC")`, or even a `SharpZipBaseException` whose message references the encryption header. We sniff the message rather than the type so a version bump doesn't silently reclassify these as generic corruption errors and produce a misleading "backup is corrupt" toast for what's really just a typo in the password.

member.name: M:VirtoCommerce.Platform.BackupRestore.BackupRestoreManager.ReportProgress(VirtoCommerce.Platform.Core.ExportImport.ExportImportProgressInfo,System.Action{VirtoCommerce.Platform.Core.ExportImport.ExportImportProgressInfo},System.String,VirtoCommerce.Platform.Core.Modularity.ProgressMessageLevel)
member.summary: Emits a structured progress message as a delta on , invokes the callback, then clears the delta so subsequent legacy callbacks (which only update ) don't re-emit it. Errors are also accumulated into the legacy list.

Invokes with try/catch around the per-section deserialization. On failure, advances back to so the outer dispatch loop resumes at the next top-level property and doesn't iterate over leftover tokens inside the failed section's array.

member.name: T:VirtoCommerce.Platform.BackupRestore.SharpZipBackupArchive
member.summary: SharpZipLib-backed implementation. Write mode uses (sequential, one entry at a time). Read mode uses SharpZipLib's ZipFile (random access by entry name) — aliased as SharpZipFile to avoid colliding with the VirtoCommerce.Platform.Data.ZipFile namespace under which this class lives.
member.remarks: Write/Read are mutually exclusive — an instance is either a writer (created via the writing constructor) or a reader (created via the reading constructor), never both.

member.name: T:VirtoCommerce.Platform.BackupRestore.SharpZipBackupArchive.EntryWriteStream
member.summary: Per-entry write stream. Delegates to the underlying ; on dispose, calls instead of closing the archive stream. The constructor's onClose callback fires after CloseEntry so the parent archive can restore encryption state.
